title: Storms and Pancakes

jokes.text: Tom's awkward response to wedding question: 'She's way out of my league. That's the correct answer.'
jokes.setup: Crowd member asks if Tom and someone will get married
jokes.punchline: Tom deflects with self-deprecation while acknowledging the 'correct' political answer
jokes.explanation: Sharp political awareness mixed with genuine humility - very Tom James character work

jokes.text: Tom's overly careful qualifier: 'I mean, within obviously acceptable propriety and so on'
jokes.setup: Tom tells crowd he loves them too
jokes.punchline: Immediately adds bureaucratic disclaimer to innocent statement
jokes.explanation: Perfect political over-caution satire - shows how politicians must qualify everything

jokes.text: Funnel cake physical comedy escalation ending with 'So fat. Thank you. No, don't talk anymore. No.'
jokes.setup: Tom makes innocent joke about not fitting through bus door after eating funnel cakes
jokes.punchline: Doubles down awkwardly explaining the fat joke, realizes he's bombing
jokes.explanation: Classic cringe escalation - shows Tom's charm failing in real time

jokes.text: 'No, what I said was Amy Brookheimer had issues, not problems. No, she got mad. She's not mentally mad.'
jokes.setup: Mike trying to clarify his previous statement about Amy
jokes.punchline: Accidentally creates more confusion with 'mad' having multiple meanings
jokes.explanation: Pure Mike McLintock - well-meaning but making things worse through poor word choice

jokes.text: No, she got mad. She's not mentally mad.
jokes.setup: Continuation of Mike's damage control
jokes.punchline: Another weak distinction that makes things worse
jokes.explanation: Mike doubling down on his semantic distinctions shows his PR incompetence

jokes.text: 'You are the sun, I the moon. Of Jupiter. Not even one of the good ones.'
jokes.setup: Tom making flowery comparison about serving Selina
jokes.punchline: Escalates self-deprecation to absurd astronomical specificity
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation with perfect specificity - 'not even one of the good ones' is pure gold

jokes.text: 'You know, a wolf is actually a pack animal. I'm more of a lone squirrel.'
jokes.setup: Article calls Tom a 'lone wolf'
jokes.punchline: Tom corrects the biology then offers hilariously diminished animal comparison
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom James - intellectually accurate but self-deprecating to absurd degree

jokes.text: Fun fact... The Wright brothers used their bicycle repair shop to make the first airplane. - I knew that.
jokes.setup: Mike delivers an unnecessary fun fact
jokes.punchline: Tom's dismissive response
jokes.explanation: Shows Mike's tendency to share useless information and Tom's polite dismissal

jokes.text: 'Fun fact... we're running a campaign here, not a bake sale.'
jokes.setup: Mike gives pointless Wright Brothers trivia
jokes.punchline: Someone sarcastically mimics Mike's 'fun fact' format to shut him down
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to Mike's annoying trivia habit - uses his own format against him

jokes.text: Selina's sexual tension delusion: 'Did you get a load of that lone squirrel comment?' 'And that means?' 'Exactly.'
jokes.setup: Selina convinced Tom is flirting with her
jokes.punchline: Gary agrees without understanding, Selina treats his confusion as confirmation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina narcissism meeting Gary's eager-to-please cluelessness

jokes.text: 'We almost... Kissed?' 'Fucked.'
jokes.setup: Selina building up to revealing past moment with Tom
jokes.punchline: Gary guesses romantic, Selina immediately goes nuclear
jokes.explanation: Sharp escalation - Gary's innocent guess vs Selina's blunt reality

jokes.text: 'And it's about to take North Carolina from behind.'
jokes.setup: Describing hurricane's approach
jokes.punchline: Uses sexual metaphor for storm trajectory
jokes.explanation: Standard innuendo but fits Ben's character - crude but functional

jokes.text: 'We have the full power of the National Weather Service, Mike.' 'Sure, but this is a really good app.'
jokes.setup: Selina points out they have professional meteorologists
jokes.punchline: Mike doubles down on his phone app
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mike McLintock - missing obvious hierarchy, trusting random technology

jokes.text: 'It's like a morgue full of dead librarians.'
jokes.setup: Dan commenting on how quiet the office is
jokes.punchline: Escalates silence metaphor to absurd specificity
jokes.explanation: Great imagery - takes familiar 'quiet as morgue' and adds perfect specific detail

jokes.text: 'This place is leaking bodies faster than a Chinese ferry.'
jokes.setup: Dan listing all the people who've left
jokes.punchline: Compares staff turnover to maritime disasters
jokes.explanation: Dark but effective metaphor - though relying on stereotypes slightly cheapens it

jokes.text: 'A joke's not a joke unless everyone can laugh at it.' Response: 'This is what I have to look forward to... A bunch of fucking half-wit jokers trying to grab my balls.'
jokes.setup: Someone defends ball-grabbing as harmless joke
jokes.punchline: Jonah points out he's the one being grabbed, not laughing
jokes.explanation: Good setup of victim's perspective vs perpetrator's rationalization - rare Jonah sympathy moment

jokes.text: 'I was veep, they polled other candidates, fucked me in my slack old ass. Sound like a best seller?'
jokes.setup: Tom suggests Selina write memoirs
jokes.punchline: Selina reduces her career to crude, bitter summary
jokes.explanation: Peak Selina bitterness - takes noble suggestion and makes it hilariously crude

jokes.text: 'I don't want to break a tit. They weren't cheap.'
jokes.setup: Selina worried about falling off bike
jokes.punchline: Reveals cosmetic surgery with casual crudeness
jokes.explanation: Classic Selina overshare - practical concern becomes TMI revelation

jokes.text: 'I did do the Tour of Italy 2008, but my mountain times were embarrassing.'
jokes.setup: Asked if he bikes much, Tom says hardly at all
jokes.punchline: Immediately reveals he did professional-level cycling
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom James humble-bragging - claims to barely bike after mentioning Tour de Italy

jokes.text: 'I should be riding in a basket in the front like E.T.'
jokes.setup: Selina notes Tom's bike is bigger than hers
jokes.punchline: References iconic movie scene about size difference
jokes.explanation: Decent pop culture reference but fairly obvious - works but not particularly clever

jokes.text: 'The only thing I can see is my eyeball. That's a great image. Vital, dynamic.'
jokes.setup: Selina complaining bike mirror doesn't work
jokes.punchline: Sarcastically calls useless eyeball view 'vital, dynamic'
jokes.explanation: Solid sarcasm about her own image problems - self-aware but not exceptional

jokes.text: Amy's text: 'You looked sweaty on TV yesterday. Are you going through the change?'
jokes.setup: Amy sending revenge texts to Selina
jokes.punchline: Hits Selina with menopause insult
jokes.explanation: Mean but effective - captures Amy's vindictive precision in targeting insecurities

jokes.text: 'I suggested that a week ago and I was shot down like a hang glider over the Pentagon.'
jokes.setup: Amy seeing pancake breakfast she previously suggested
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific metaphor for being rejected
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific metaphor - hang glider over Pentagon perfectly captures hopeless situation

jokes.text: 'I have more White House staff than the White House. And a whiter building.'
jokes.setup: Sidney showing off his collection of ex-White House staff
jokes.punchline: Plays on 'White House' literally and figuratively
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay with political edge - works but not groundbreaking

jokes.text: 'I'm like Picasso and I'm stuck painting fences.'
jokes.setup: Jonah complaining his talents are wasted
jokes.punchline: Compares himself to artistic genius while doing menial work
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jonah delusion - comparing himself to Picasso while being incompetent

jokes.text: 'VP Doyle's a lame duck. And you know what you do with lame animals?' 'You care for them.' 'You shoot them dead.'
jokes.setup: Jonah setting up obvious cruel punchline
jokes.punchline: Richard's innocent response highlights Jonah's callousness
jokes.explanation: Great character contrast - Richard's sweetness vs Jonah's political brutality

jokes.text: 'Not co... I don't get corns.'
jokes.setup: Selina trying to remember details of romantic moment
jokes.punchline: Gets defensive about foot condition during seduction attempt
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina priority confusion - vanity trumps romance in crucial moment

jokes.text: the cold got to us and then you decided... you wanted to walk home. Yeah, I love to walk at night.
jokes.setup: Selina building up to their romantic moment
jokes.punchline: Tom completely missing the innuendo and talking about actual walking
jokes.explanation: Masterful deflation - Selina's loaded romantic setup meets Tom's oblivious practicality

jokes.text: 'You're a guy, okay? You can't have any idea what I'm talking about. That's right, I am a guy. And Tom's a guy. You want me to talk to him like guy on guy?'
jokes.setup: Selina dismissing Gary's understanding of her humiliation
jokes.punchline: Gary's innocent offer sounds sexual
jokes.explanation: Accidental innuendo from Gary's eagerness to help - modest but functional

jokes.text: 'I'm going commando at tomorrow's pancake brunch.' 'What? No underwear?' 'What? No. Jesus Christ. Yeah, that'll win me the election. A nice shot of my beav.'
jokes.setup: Selina using military term Gary misunderstands
jokes.punchline: Selina horrified at implication, creates worse mental image
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection followed by Selina's characteristic crude panic

jokes.text: 'Dan Egan is back. Like denim and measles.'
jokes.setup: Dan announcing his return
jokes.punchline: Compares himself to things that come back (one good, one bad)
jokes.explanation: Clever pairing - denim (fashionable comeback) with measles (unwanted return)

jokes.text: 'I hope your vagina falls off.'
jokes.setup: Amy talking to Nicola at Dan's party
jokes.punchline: Drops nuclear curse mid-conversation
jokes.explanation: Shocking escalation that perfectly captures Amy's mental state post-White House

jokes.text: it chews you up and it shits you out and then you are left with nothing but the night sweats and flashbacks.
jokes.setup: Amy describing how DC treats people
jokes.punchline: Escalating to PTSD-like symptoms from politics
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing the psychological toll of politics on Amy

jokes.text: 'Am I the only man that he...' 'As far as we know.'
jokes.setup: Jonah learning about sexual harassment lawsuit
jokes.punchline: Discovers his unique status as male victim
jokes.explanation: Awkward moment of Jonah's special victimhood - uncomfortable but character-appropriate

jokes.text: 'Jesus fucking Christ. You all look like me. This is a nightmare. It's a fucking nightmare.'
jokes.setup: Jonah sees room full of tall women
jokes.punchline: Horrified recognition of physical similarity
jokes.explanation: Perfect visual gag setup - Jonah's horror at seeing feminine versions of himself

jokes.text: Jesus fucking Christ. You all look like me. This is a nightmare. It's a fucking nightmare.
jokes.setup: Jonah in a room full of tall women harassment victims
jokes.punchline: His horror at seeing people who resemble him
jokes.explanation: Great visual comedy and character moment - Jonah's vanity meeting harsh reality

jokes.text: 'Well, you do have that one purple shirt that looks a bit blousy, but...'
jokes.setup: Jonah asks if he looks like a middle-aged woman
jokes.punchline: Richard innocently provides specific evidence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Richard obliviousness - helpful answer to question Jonah didn't want answered

jokes.text: It's just it's his right ear that's freakishly large. No, his right, my left.
jokes.setup: Gary helping Selina remember Congressman Cheever
jokes.punchline: Confusion over which ear is deformed
jokes.explanation: Gary's helpful confusion about spatial relationships and physical deformities

jokes.text: 'No, you flip when you see holes. Bubbles is too soon. Holes, not bubbles.'
jokes.setup: Mike getting pancake instructions wrong
jokes.punchline: Increasingly urgent correction about pancake technique
jokes.explanation: Minor moment showing Mike's incompetence at basic tasks

jokes.text: 'Fun fact... scopolamine was first used as a truth serum by the Czechoslovakian state police. How is that fun?'
jokes.setup: Mike giving another inappropriate 'fun fact'
jokes.punchline: Selina finally questions the 'fun' in disturbing trivia
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback - finally someone questions Mike's disturbing 'fun' facts

jokes.text: 'Well... it's either surprisingly little or surprisingly a lot.'
jokes.setup: Mike asked about honeybee honey production
jokes.punchline: Completely useless non-answer after building expectation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mike McLintock - confident delivery of completely useless information

jokes.text: 'This is just a Swiss suicide clinic with a hint of jojoba.'
jokes.setup: Amy at relaxing spa
jokes.punchline: Compares peaceful spa to assisted dying facility
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark observation - perfectly captures Amy's inability to relax in any context

jokes.text: 'Which, when you have a bag instead of a bowel, is not good.'
jokes.setup: Bernie explaining why he can't have dairy
jokes.punchline: Overshares about colostomy bag at pancake breakfast
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable overshare at inappropriate moment - effective cringe but not exceptional

jokes.text: Which, when you have a bag instead of a bowel, is not good.
jokes.setup: Bernie mentioning cow's milk makes him gassy
jokes.punchline: Revealing he has a colostomy bag
jokes.explanation: Medical TMI for cringe effect, somewhat predictable but effective

jokes.text: 'Who's, uh... who's achin' for some bacon?'
jokes.setup: Tom trying to use corny line Mike suggested for Selina
jokes.punchline: Awkward delivery of terrible pun
jokes.explanation: Callback showing Tom's discomfort with corniness - lands because it's clearly not him

jokes.text: 'Well, spoils of war, handsome. You want top clients, be more like Amy. Or better.'
jokes.setup: Dan complaining about losing clients to Amy
jokes.punchline: Sidney dismisses him with backhanded compliment
jokes.explanation: Effective putdown - shows Dan's reduced status in new environment

jokes.text: 'You are Beyoncé. He is backup bootie.'
jokes.setup: Gary trying to boost Selina's confidence
jokes.punchline: Awkward attempt at hip compliment
jokes.explanation: Gary trying to be cool and failing - 'backup bootie' is awkward but sweet

jokes.text: You listen to me. You are Beyoncé. He is backup bootie.
jokes.setup: Selina feeling insecure about Tom's pancake skills
jokes.punchline: Gary's attempt to boost her confidence with pop culture comparison
jokes.explanation: Gary's motivational speech using contemporary pop culture metaphors

jokes.text: 'Ma'am, you've got to get back to DC faster than a speeding drunk.'
jokes.setup: Ben saying they need to hurry back
jokes.punchline: Modifies Superman reference to alcoholic
jokes.explanation: Good twist on familiar phrase - drunk driver as speed reference is darkly funny

jokes.text: 'Yeah, six hours till flying cow time.'
jokes.setup: Describing severity of approaching hurricane
jokes.punchline: References Twister movie imagery of airborne livestock
jokes.explanation: Pop culture reference that effectively communicates hurricane severity with humor

jokes.text: 'Hell, yes. I mean, no offense meant, Sue, but when it's just you and me, it's like it's just me.'
jokes.setup: Ben excited about everyone returning from campaign
jokes.punchline: Accidentally reveals Sue's complete lack of personality
jokes.explanation: Brutal but perfect character observation - Ben's honesty about Sue's non-presence

jokes.text: 'I have had it with her category five fucking snoot.'
jokes.setup: Tom frustrated with governor during hurricane meeting
jokes.punchline: Uses hurricane scale to describe attitude
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay - uses hurricane terminology for personality complaint, perfectly timed

jokes.text: It was actually free. I updated my software and it just appeared on my screen. And I was like, 'What is this thing?' And I just picked it up.
jokes.setup: Mike's weather app being wrong about the storm
jokes.punchline: His complete ignorance about how he got the app
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mike - complete technological incompetence delivered with matter-of-fact confidence

jokes.text: 'It fell at the first fence and it got shot. And now some French fucker's got it in a baguette.'
jokes.setup: Selina's failed hurricane gamble compared to horse racing
jokes.punchline: Escalates metaphor to absurd culinary conclusion
jokes.explanation: Exceptional escalation - takes horse metaphor to completely unexpected culinary endpoint. Peak Veep writing.

jokes.text: 'A number of tall women were molested and Mr. Ryan was one of them.'
jokes.setup: Richard explaining Jonah's harassment case
jokes.punchline: Innocently groups Jonah with women victims
jokes.explanation: Perfect Richard innocence - matter-of-fact delivery makes Jonah's situation more absurd

jokes.text: 'This isn't damage. Just some shitty building.'
jokes.setup: Viewing supposed hurricane damage for photo op
jokes.punchline: Reveals 'damage' is just poor construction
jokes.explanation: Solid observation about fake photo op - reveals political theater

jokes.text: O'Brien just pulled a teddy bear out of the ruins of a house.
jokes.setup: Looking for storm damage for photo ops
jokes.punchline: Competitor getting perfect disaster photo opportunity
jokes.explanation: Good observation about political photo op competition, efficiently delivered

jokes.text: Come on, let's go. You don't have to say hi to them. We've already said hi to these folks. Come on.
jokes.setup: Tom successfully engaging with workers
jokes.punchline: Selina's impatient dismissal of constituents
jokes.explanation: Shows Selina's impatience and lack of genuine interest in people, character-appropriate but not exceptional

tmdb_overview: Selina is annoyed by the press' reaction to her running mate's charm.
insight: Cringe-heavy 58-joke episode leans harder on discomfort than Veep's typical satire strength.
